result:
Please provide the *full text* you would like me to summarize. The text currently provided is incomplete ("Photosynthesis converts sunlight into energy...").

Once you provide the complete text, I will create 5-6 short bullet point study notes for you!

notes:
Question 1. (2 points)
A) Analyze the distribution of death events cases among the patients and the race. calculate their respective numbers and percentages. (0.5 points)
Calculate total death and then use that to find the percentage.

B) Compute descriptive statistics of the data. Comment on the results. (0.75 points)
df.describe()

C) Analyze the skew and the kurtosis of medical variables distributions(Numbers & Graphs). Comment on the results. (0.75 points)
Kurtosis: how often outliers occur
Skewness: the degree of asymmetry in a probability distribution
KDE is used to visualize the probability density of a continuous variable.

Question 2. (2 points)
Use univariate plots to analyze patterns in each of the medical variables with respect to the outcome (death status). What categories of patients( race and marital status) were most likely to die from breast cancer? . (2 points)
Univariate plots are used to analyze stats one variable at a time.
Use a histogram for numerical data
Use a count plot(bar chart) for categorial data

Question 3. (2 points)
Use multivariate plots to:
A) Analyze the relationship between medical variables. Comment on the results (1 points)
Use a correlation matrix due to multiple variables
annot=True, annotates the cell with the numerical value
cmap='viridis', built-in colour map
fmt=".2f", specfies the text is rouned to 2 decimal places

B) Identify potential factors that can predict Death event in married patients. Comment on the results. (1 points)
Use a pair plot, box plot

Question 4. (3 points)
Identify and remove any outliers in the medical variables. Explain the rationale for identifying and removing outliers. (3 points)
Remove outliers by setting a lower and higher bound.
Using a Interquartile range
Remove outliers to uphold the integrity of the data

Question 5. (3 points)
How did you treat missing values for the attributes that you included in the analysis ? Provide a detailed explanation in the comments.(3 points)
fillna()
For numerical values you substitute missing values with the mean in that column
For categorial values you substitute missing values with the first mode in that column

Question 6. (3 points)
The dataset contains contains alot of zeros, identify which attributes cannot be 0 medically speaking, and impute them, while explaining the rational behind it.(3 points)
Tumor size of zero means patient doesn’t have cancer
 Regional node count of zero suggests a lack of examination, raising questions about how the cancer diagnosis was set up.
 Lastly, a regional node positive count of zero would indicate the absence of cancer in the patient.

Question 7. (2 points)
Identify the attibutes that will need rescalling ( with explanation ), apply one of the rescalling techniques we have seen in our course and explain your rational. (2 points)
Tumor size and survival months will need to be rescaled to ensure they have comparable ranges.
Re-scaling technique: min-max  
MinMaxScaler()
-	Ensures that all features are on the same scale
-	Preserves the relationship between features by providing values between 1 and 0.
-	Easy

Question 8. (3 points)
Through extensive research, provide additional attributes that you can create that will enhance your dataset, explain the logic behind it, and add them to your data, (1.5 points)
Create a score for cancer assessment to understand numerically the extent of the cancer’s progression.
	(Reginol node positive / reginol code examined) * tumor size.

identify the most irrelevant attribute(s), and exclude them from your project for the next steps (1.5 points)
df.drop(): race and marital status




Assignment 2
Important Features for classification:
Age', 'T Stage ', 'N Stage', 'Grade', 'A Stage', 'Tumor Size', 'Estrogen Status', 'Progesterone Status', 'Regional Node Examined', 'Reginol Node Positive', 'differentiate', 'Metastasis Risk Score'
Important features for regression:
'Age', 'Tumor Size', 'Regional Node Examined', 'Reginol Node Positive'

Data Preparation & Spliting for the Regression Model
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

classification model:
LogisticRegression: predicts one or two possible outcomes

Regression Model:
RandomForestRegressor()-uses multiple decision trees, provides estimated feature importance
LinearRegression()- mdoels a relationship between the dependent variable (target) and one or more independent variables

notes:
Assignment 4 Refactor Plan

Collections that will change
The shifts collection will change by embedding employee ObjectId values inside an employees array instead of referencing assignments through a separate collection.
The employees collection will remove the employeeId field and rely only on MongoDB ObjectId (_id).
The assignments collection will be removed completely because shift documents will now store employee references directly.

Persistence functions that must be modified or removed
Functions that previously accessed assignment data must be removed.
Functions that retrieved shift assignments must now read employee ObjectIds from the employees array inside each shift document.
Functions that created assignments must instead push employee ObjectIds directly into the shift employees array.
Functions that used employeeId or shiftId must be updated to use MongoDB ObjectId values.

Changes required to business logic functions
Business logic that previously depended on assignment relationships must be rewritten to work with embedded employee arrays.
Shift creation logic must initialize an empty employees array.
Employee assignment logic must insert ObjectIds into the shift employees array instead of inserting documents into the assignments collection.
Employee lookup operations must convert string ids into ObjectId format.

Effect of embedded arrays on update operations
Embedded arrays simplify lookup operations because assignments can be retrieved directly from the shift document.
Update operations must now modify arrays using push and pull operations.
Deleting employees from shifts requires removing ObjectIds from the employees array instead of deleting assignment documents.

Risks or anticipated complications
Existing references using employeeId and shiftId may break if not fully replaced.
String ids must be converted to ObjectId format before database queries.
Application routes that depended on assignment collection joins must be updated.
Testing is required to confirm that all shift assignment functionality continues working after migration.

• Build: Creating prototypes (from low to high fidelity) that can be tested.

feedback.

notes:
3

Automatic Zoom
CI/CD Pipeline — Delivering the App
Build the pipeline that ships a containerised AI quiz app from code to production. Every git push
to main automatically builds, tests, and deploys — no manual steps.
1. Overview
You are given a fully-working quiz app integrated with Gemini 2.5 Flash. The Python code,
frontend, and database layer are complete. Your task is to write two files from scratch — a
Dockerfile to containerise the app, and a GitHub Actions workflow to automate its delivery —
and configure the cloud services that run the pipeline end to end.
The app includes a placeholder that displays your STUDENT_ID environment variable. This
must be visible in every screenshot you submit.
What you will build
git push → GitHub Actions → Docker Hub → Render (live URL)
2. Prerequisites
Set up everything below before starting. All accounts are free.
Account / Tool Action needed
GitHub Sign up at github.com. Create a new empty public repository for this
assignment.
Docker Hub Sign up at hub.docker.com. Note your username — it will be a GitHub
Secret.
Render Sign up at render.com. Use your GitHub account for easy login.
Git Verify: git --version. Install from git-scm.com if missing.
Docker Desktop Verify: docker --version. Install from docs.docker.com/get-docker if
missing.
Python 3.11+ Verify: python3 --version. Install from python.org if missing.
Gemini API key Provided via the course portal. Keep it private — never commit it.
3. Starter Code
Download and unzip the starter pack. It contains:
assign8-starter/
├── static/
│ ├── index.html
│ ├── styles.css
│ └── app.js
├── app.py Flask routes + /api/info endpoint (reads STUDENT_ID)
├── ai.py Gemini 2.5 Flash integration
├── db.py SQLite schema
├── requirements.txt
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
# You must create these two files yourself:
# Dockerfile
# .github/workflows/ci-cd.yml
⚠ Never commit .env or your API key.
.env is already in .gitignore. Run git status before every push and confirm .env is not
listed.
Part 1: Run the App Locally ·
⏱ 20 min
Verify the app works before containerising it.
1.1 Install and configure
pip install -r requirements.txt
# Create your .env file from the example
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env: set GEMINI_KEY (from D2L) and STUDENT_ID (e.g. s1234567)
1.2 Run and verify
python app.py
# Open http://localhost:5000
# Your student ID should appear in the page
Checkpoint 1
Page shows your student ID
/api/health returns {"status": "ok"}
You can generate a quiz and answer questions
"Ask AI to Explain" and "Get Study Recommendations" return real AI responses
1.3 Verify the database
Complete at least one full quiz session in the browser, then confirm the data was written to
SQLite. You can use either method below — pick whichever you prefer.
Option A — Python shell
python3
>>> import db
>>> conn = db.get_connection()
>>> conn.execute("SELECT * FROM sessions").fetchall()
[(1, 'Docker and Containers', 'intermediate', ...)] # at least one row
>>> conn.execute("SELECT * FROM answers").fetchall()
[(1, 1, 1, 'Docker and Containers', 2, 1), ...] # several rows
>>> conn.close()
>>> exit()
Option B — sqlite3 CLI
sqlite3 quiz.db
sqlite> SELECT * FROM sessions;
1|Docker and Containers|intermediate|2024-...
sqlite> SELECT * FROM answers;
1|1|1|Docker and Containers|2|1
2|1|2|Docker and Containers|0|0
...
sqlite> .quit
sqlite3 not installed?
On Mac: brew install sqlite3 · On Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install sqlite3 ·
On Windows: download from sqlite.org/download.html
Checkpoint 1b
sessions table has at least one row
answers table has several rows (one per question answered)
Take a screenshot of this terminal output — it is required in the submission template
Part 2: Write the Dockerfile ·
⏱ 30 min
Create a file named `Dockerfile` in the root of your project. It must satisfy the specification
below. You are not given the file — look up the Dockerfile reference at
docs.docker.com/reference/dockerfile/ as needed or use the demo app, available here:
https://github.com/learn-cicd/cicd-demo
Requirement Specification Notes
Base image python:3.11-slim Do not use a newer or heavier
image
Working directory /app All subsequent commands run
here
Layer caching Copy requirements.txt first, then
pip install
Avoids reinstalling packages on
every code change
Install flags --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt Keeps image size small
Application files Copy everything else after pip
install
Order matters for cache efficiency
DB initialisation python -c "import db;
db.init_db()"
Run at build time so the schema
exists on startup
Port variable ENV PORT=5000 Render overrides this at runtime
Requirement Specification Notes
Start command gunicorn, bind
0.0.0.0:$PORT, 2 workers,
app:app
Use shell-form CMD so $PORT
expands
Shell-form vs exec-form CMD
CMD ["gunicorn", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:$PORT", ...] will NOT expand $PORT. Use
shell form: CMD ["sh", "-c", "gunicorn --bind 0.0.0.0:$PORT ..."].
2.2 Build and test the container locally
docker build -t devops-quiz-ai .
# Run the container with environment variables from .env file
docker run -d -p 5001:5000 --name quiz-ai --env-file .env quiz-ai
# Alternatively, pass environment variables directly
docker run -d -p 5001:5000 \
-e GEMINI_KEY=your_key \
-e STUDENT_ID=s1234567 \
--name quiz-ai \
quiz-ai
# Open http://localhost:5001 — app should work identically to python app.py
# Footer must still show your student ID
Checkpoint 2
docker build completes with no errors
Container starts and app is accessible at http://localhost:5000
Footer shows your student ID inside the container
AI features work (Gemini key passed via -e flag)
Part 3: Write the CI/CD Workflow ·
⏱
40 min
Create the file .github/workflows/ci-cd.yml (you will need to create the .github/workflows/
directories). The workflow must satisfy the specification below. Refer to the GitHub Actions
documentation at docs.github.com/en/actions and the Docker Hub action docs at
github.com/marketplace. Alternatively, use the demo app showcased in the lecture session:
https://github.com/learn-cicd/cicd-demo.
3.1 Triggers
Event Branches Effect
push main Full pipeline: build → smoke test
→ push → deploy
pull_request main CI only: build + smoke test (no
push, no deploy)
3.2 Job 1 — build-test-push (runs-on: ubuntu-latest)
Step # Step name How to implement Key parameters / notes
1 Checkout code Use
actions/checkout@v4
—
2 Log in to Docker Hub Use docker/login-
action@v3
username:
DOCKERHUB_USERNAME
secret password:
DOCKERHUB_TOKEN
secret
3 Build image (no push
yet)
Use docker/build-push-
action@v5
push: false tags:
USERNAME/devops-quiz-
ai:latest
USERNAME/devops-quiz-
ai:${{ github.sha }}
4 Smoke test Shell run block Start container with docker
run -d on port 5000 wait 5 s
curl /api/health → check
response contains "ok" stop
container
5 Push to Docker Hub Use docker/build-push-
action@v5
push: true Same two
tags as step 3
Condition: if:
github.event_name ==
'push'
3.3 Job 2 — deploy (runs-on: ubuntu-latest)
Property Value
needs build-test-push
if github.event_name == 'push'
Step Single run block: curl POST to ${{
secrets.RENDER_DEPLOY_URL }}
3.4 Required GitHub Secrets
Add these in your repository under Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New
repository secret:
Secret name Value / where to find it
DOCKERHUB_USERNAME Your Docker Hub username (e.g. jsmith42)
